settings_move_conflict_title: There is already a library there
settings_move_failed:
Your library could not be moved, so it has been left exactly where it was. The file in the way:

{files}
settings_colour_from_desktop: Every colour here is worked out from your desktop's accent colour ({colour}), by Material Design 3's rules. Change it in your system settings and this app follows.
settings_colour_own: Every colour here is worked out from {colour}, by Material Design 3's rules. Your desktop has no accent colour to follow.
settings_cache_cleared: {n} cover(s) removed. They download again as you need them.
about_licence: This program comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. It is free software, and you are welcome to redistribute it under the terms of the GNU General Public License, version 3 or later.
welcome_line: Your books, your shelf, on your own computer.
setup_title: Where should your books be kept?
setup_explain: Pick a folder. Your library is a plain file inside it, so you can back it up or move it yourself whenever you like.
ol_offer: Open Library had no record of {title}. You just entered it by hand — would you like to contribute it, so the next person finds it?
ol_password: Password (sent to Open Library, never stored in a file):
ol_remember: Keep it in this computer's own credential store, so you are not asked again? It is never written to a file.
ol_thanks: Added to Open Library. Thank you for contributing.
ol_login: Open Library did not accept that sign-in. Your book is safe in your library; only the contribution did not go through.
ol_denied: Your Open Library account is not allowed to import books — most are not. Your book is safe in your library; nothing else is wrong.
ol_network: Open Library could not be reached. Your book is safe in your library; you can try contributing it again later.
ol_failed: Open Library could not add the book this time. It is safe in your library regardless.
isbn_hint: Type an ISBN and press Enter
fetch_not_found: No book with that ISBN. You can still type it in yourself.
isbn_length: An ISBN is 10 or 13 digits. That one has {n}.
isbn_checksum10: That is 10 digits, but the last one does not check out. It is there to catch exactly this: a digit typed wrong, or two swapped round. Read it off the book again.
isbn_checksum13: That is 13 digits, but the last one does not check out. It is there to catch exactly this: a digit typed wrong, or two swapped round. Read it off the book again.
isbn_refused:
{why}

The book has not been saved. Correct the ISBN, or empty the box to save the book without one.
save_failed:
Your library could not be written to:
{path}
search_hint: Search by title, author or ISBN…
empty_library: No books yet. Add one from the Add Book page.
damaged_title: Your library file could not be read
damaged:
{path}

It could not be read, so it has been set aside under the name above and the app started with an empty library. Your books are still in that file — nothing was deleted. Repair it or restore a copy, then start again.
export_failed:
The file could not be written:
{path}
template_failed: That file could not be written.
import_unreadable_title: That file could not be read
import_unreadable: It is not a spreadsheet this app can open.
import_none: That file has no ISBNs in it.
import_not_found:
These were not found, and were skipped. You can add them by hand on this page:

{isbns}
lend_pick_person: Pick who it is going to.
lend_failed:
The loan could not be saved to:
{path}
person_pick_first: Pick somebody from the list first.
person_remove_confirm: Remove {name}? What they have borrowed stays in the history.
history_none: Pick somebody to see what they have borrowed.
people_empty:
Nobody yet.
Add somebody, and you can start lending books out.
stats_subtitle: Worked out from the {n} book(s) in your library.
stats_empty: Add some books and start reading — the numbers turn up here.
chart_time_empty: No book has both a start and a finish date in this window yet, so there is no time to divide up.
all_here: All {n} copies are on your shelf.
some_here: {here} of {total} copies are on your shelf.
copies_too_few: {out} of them are in somebody's hands, so you cannot have fewer than that.
copies_added: You already had {title}, so it is now {n} copies rather than a second entry.
delete_all_sure:
Delete every copy, and the book with them?

This cannot be undone.
delete_while_out: {names} still has a copy. The lending history is kept either way.
